testBaseline.note: Baseline from Sprint 11 close (7/7 stories, B-037 Walk phase COMPLETE, merged to main@0ffcd47). 2068 Windows fast-suite passed. Pi aarch64 baseline from Sprint 10 US-182: 1583 passed + 47 pi_only on chi-eclipse-01. US-187 rename will touch ~45 files and MUST preserve this count exactly.

sprintNotes: This sprint is polish + hygiene, not a Crawl/Walk/Run phase. Goal: clear the three Sprint 10/11 follow-on items (US-165 display advanced tier, US-183 pygame HDMI render, B-042/US-187 obd package rename) plus the long-standing Pylint CI noise (US-186). Sets up a clean tree for Run phase once CIO completes BT pairing + car mount.

AGENT MODEL: Hybrid (same as Sprint 10/11). Ralph writes code on Windows + SSHes to mcornelison@10.27.27.28 for Pi-side validation. Pi-side work this sprint is limited to US-183 (physical display on HDMI -- needs CIO to eyeball output) and US-187 smoke-test (`python -c 'import obd; print(obd.OBD)'` in the Pi venv). Other stories run on Windows.
SERVER STATE ON SPRINT START: chi-srv-01:8000 healthy. No server changes this sprint -- all work is Pi-side or CI.

PI STATE ON SPRINT START: Sprint 11 shipped 7/7. Pi runs full sync pipeline to chi-srv-01 in simulator mode. systemd service starts clean via BL-004 fix. UpsMonitor returns valid power-source via VCELL trend. Bench-only; no BT, no car.

SPRINT-BRANCH WORKFLOW (Rule 8): runs on sprint/pi-polish (created from main by PM). Marcus committed this sprint setup. Ralph leaves all code unstaged; Marcus commits at sprint close + merges to main.

CRITICAL SEQUENCING: US-187 (obd -> obdii rename) must land BEFORE any other story that edits src/pi/* because the rename touches ~45 files and any parallel edit to src/pi/obd/ will produce merge conflicts. Recommended order: US-186 first (trivial, S, stops the email flood immediately), US-187 second (wide rename, MUST run solo in the working tree), then US-165 + US-183 in either order. If Ralph is mid-sprint and US-187 hasn't landed, he should finish whatever he's on and THEN start US-187 -- don't interleave.
STOP EARLY IF SSH BREAKS for US-183/US-187 smoke tests. Windows-only work in US-186 + US-165 + US-187 rename itself is not SSH-dependent.

DECISION CAPTURED 2026-04-18 (PM): US-186 Pylint workflow resolution = DELETE the workflow. Rationale: ruff + mypy via make pre-commit already cover lint; pylint duplicates without adding coverage; workflow predates project standards (default snake_case vs camelCase, Python 3.8/9/10 vs 3.11+, no deps install). Keeping it would require writing a .pylintrc + matrix fix + dep install -- more work than value, since ruff is already canonical. The story body authorizes Option A (delete) as default; if Ralph sees a specific reason to prefer narrow-config instead, flag to PM.
TEST COUNT INVARIANT: Sprint 11 ended at 2068 Windows fast-suite. US-187 rename is MECHANICAL -- expected post-rename count is exactly 2068 (zero regressions). Any drift triggers stopCondition.

SPOOL TOUCHPOINT: US-165 adds min/max markers + color-coded thresholds to the display. Spool Gate 2 review is a sprint-close step -- Ralph drops a review packet in offices/tuner/inbox/ with screenshots (or sim captures if no physical display) + threshold-to-color mapping. Spool responds in-lane OR via inbox note.

stories.title: Delete Pylint GitHub Actions workflow (stop the email flood)
stories.intent: Stop the Pylint workflow that's been failing on every commit since the repo was created. Root cause: default config checks snake_case but project uses camelCase, matrix includes Python 3.8/9/10 but code uses 3.11+ idioms (datetime.UTC), and the workflow installs only pylint without the project's actual requirements. Pylint duplicates ruff (already in use via make pre-commit) without adding coverage. Default resolution: DELETE the workflow. If Ralph sees a strong reason to prefer narrow-config instead, flag to PM before proceeding.

filesToTouch: .github/workflows/pylint.yml (DELETE)
.github/workflows/README.md (create a one-liner noting ruff + mypy are the canonical lint path -- optional but nice)
docs/cross-platform-development.md or specs/methodology.md (if either references the Pylint workflow -- delete those references)

acceptance: git rm .github/workflows/pylint.yml (delete the failing workflow)
Next push to main (or sprint branch) triggers ONLY the expected workflows -- no Pylint run
gh run list -R mcornelison/OBD2 --limit 5 shows no Pylint entry for the post-merge commit
Optional: add a short .github/workflows/README.md noting 'Lint runs locally via make pre-commit (ruff + mypy). No CI lint workflow -- ruff is canonical.'
Optional: grep for 'pylint' in docs/ or specs/ -- remove any stale references pointing to the deleted workflow
Completion note: link to the first post-merge commit showing no Pylint failure in the checks summary, and (if applicable) the cleaned-up docs
verification: Post-merge: gh run list --branch main -R mcornelison/OBD2 | head -10 (no Pylint entries)
invariants: Do NOT add a replacement Pylint workflow -- ruff is the canonical lint. If CI lint is wanted later, PM will groom a ruff-based CI story.
Do NOT modify .pylintrc or add one -- the rename answer is 'no Pylint in CI'
Do NOT touch src/ or tests/ -- this is a CI-only change
stopConditions: STOP if there's an argument to KEEP pylint as a narrow import-error-only check (E0401/E0611) -- surface to PM so we can decide on narrow-config vs. delete
STOP if deleting the workflow somehow breaks a branch-protection rule (Pylint may be set as required) -- check repo settings in GitHub, flag to PM for a quick remove-from-required-checks step

stories.title: Rename src/pi/obd/ → src/pi/obdii/ (B-042 fulfillment, closes I-014)
stories.intent: Resolve the third-party package name collision. Project's src/pi/obd/ package shadows python-OBD under systemd, causing obd.OBD(...) to fail with 'module obd has no attribute OBD' at Pi runtime. This has been a known landmine since Session 20 (I-014 filed). Rename to obdii unblocks Run-phase BT-pairing work (Sprint 13+). Purely mechanical: rename the directory, update all imports, update all docs.

filesToTouch: src/pi/obd/ -> src/pi/obdii/ (git mv the directory)
All src/**/*.py files with 'from pi.obd.' or 'import pi.obd.'
All tests/**/*.py files with 'from pi.obd.' or 'import pi.obd.'
All scripts/**/*.py files with pi.obd imports
specs/architecture.md (mentions of src/pi/obd)
specs/standards.md (if it uses pi.obd as an example)
CLAUDE.md + offices/ralph/agent.md (package-path references)
offices/pm/backlog/B-042-rename-obd-package-to-obdii.md (mark Resolved + link to this commit)
offices/pm/issues/014-obd-package-name-shadowing.md (mark Resolved)
deploy/eclipse-obd.service (only if ExecStart or Environment references pi.obd -- likely not)
doNotTouch: requirements*.txt (python-OBD is the one that stays as 'obd' -- do not change)

groundingRefs.unit: estimated file count for the rename blast radius (from B-042 filing)
Windows fast-suite test count invariant (zero-regression target)

acceptance: git mv src/pi/obd/ src/pi/obdii/ as the first rename step (preserves git history, avoids the file-delete-and-recreate pattern)
All 'from pi.obd.' imports rewritten to 'from pi.obdii.' across src/, tests/, scripts/, and offices/ralph/ if any
All 'import pi.obd' imports (non-from form) rewritten similarly
Any stale 'from src.pi.obd.' imports (if any survived TD-014) rewritten to 'from pi.obdii.' — TD-014 pattern is from pi.*, NOT from src.pi.*
Verify grep -r 'pi\.obd\b' src/ tests/ scripts/ offices/ralph/ returns zero matches (word boundary avoids false positives on 'obdii')

Verify grep -r "'obd'" src/ tests/ scripts/ returns no hits EXCEPT: (a) python-OBD library imports like 'import obd' at call sites that use obd.OBD(...), (b) grep false-positives in unrelated strings. Any project-package 'obd' string literals (dotted path references) must be updated
pytest tests/ on Windows passes 2068 tests (zero regressions) -- this is the MECHANICAL-RENAME invariant
ruff check src/pi/ tests/ scripts/ clean

Pi smoke test: ssh mcornelison@10.27.27.28 '~/obd2-venv/bin/python -c "import obd; print(obd.OBD)"' succeeds -- proves python-OBD library resolves correctly post-rename. Does NOT require the OBDLink LX to be present or paired.
Update specs/architecture.md Pi tier section (replace 'pi/obd/' with 'pi/obdii/' everywhere the path is referenced). If a diagram uses the old name, update
Update CLAUDE.md + offices/ralph/agent.md references
Mark offices/pm/backlog/B-042-rename-obd-package-to-obdii.md Status: Resolved with a link to this commit hash. Mark offices/pm/issues/014-obd-package-name-shadowing.md same
Completion note: file count changed, import-line count changed, test count before/after, ruff result, Pi smoke-test output
verification: pytest tests/ -v on Windows (2068 test count target, zero regressions)
pytest tests/pi/ -v on Pi (pi_only + standard, counts match Sprint 10/11 baseline)
grep -r 'pi\.obd\b' src/ tests/ scripts/ offices/ralph/ (expect zero matches)
ssh mcornelison@10.27.27.28 '~/obd2-venv/bin/python -c "import obd; print(type(obd.OBD))"' (expect <class 'obd.obd.OBD'> or similar -- proves python-OBD resolves)
invariants: MECHANICAL RENAME ONLY -- no behavioral changes, no refactors, no new features
Test count before == test count after (2068 Windows; Sprint 10/11 count on Pi)
Do NOT rename python-OBD library imports (the third-party 'obd' package stays as 'obd' -- that's the whole point)
Do NOT restructure the package contents during the rename -- module boundaries stay identical, only the directory name changes
Single commit preferred -- half-renamed tree is broken; don't ship a staged rename as separate commits
stopConditions: STOP if test count changes after the rename -- that indicates a non-mechanical side effect; surface and diagnose rather than chasing tests
STOP if grep reveals a string-literal reference to 'pi.obd' in a config or data file that isn't a .py source -- those are dynamic imports and need manual review (B-042 risk section flagged this)
STOP if Pi smoke test still fails after rename -- the whole point of this story is to fix that; if it still fails, surface full output to PM and do not try to patch ad hoc

stories.title: Display advanced tier — connectivity indicators + min/max markers + color-coded thresholds
stories.intent: Extend the primary display beyond the basic-tier gauges (US-164) with three connection indicators (OBD/WiFi/Sync), min/max markers from recent drives, and tiered-threshold color coding. Spool Gate 2 review checkpoint. Uses simulator data for validation -- real-drive data review waits for Run phase.

filesToTouch: src/pi/display/screens/primary_renderer.py (extend existing basic-tier renderer)
src/pi/display/screens/primary_screen.py (if connectivity indicators land at screen level vs renderer level)
src/pi/display/state/ (if new state holders are needed for min/max markers + connection state)
src/pi/display/theme.py or similar (color-coded threshold mapping)
tests/pi/display/test_primary_screen_render.py (extend)
tests/pi/display/test_primary_screen_basic_tier.py (add advanced-tier test file or extend)
specs/architecture.md (display advanced tier section)
offices/tuner/inbox/2026-04-18-from-marcus-us165-gate2-review.md (Spool Gate 2 review packet at sprint close)
filesToRead: specs/obd2-research.md (safe operating ranges for threshold color mapping)
doNotTouch: src/pi/obd/ (awaits US-187 rename -- if Ralph runs US-165 after US-187, this becomes src/pi/obdii/)

groundingRefs.value: RPM, Coolant, Boost, AFR, Speed, Battery Voltage

groundingRefs.unit: cold / normal / caution / danger color bands

acceptance: Header shows three connection indicators: OBD, WiFi, Sync. Green = connected/recent, gray = disconnected, red = error. Data source: OBD from pi.obdii.* (post-US-187 rename), WiFi from network state, Sync from sync_log last_synced_at recency
Each gauge displays min/max markers from recent drives in brackets (e.g., 'RPM 2400 [min 780 / max 6200]'). 'Recent' = last 5 drives per default; configurable in config.json

Gauge values color-coded per threshold tier: blue = below normal operating, white = normal, orange = caution/WATCH, red = danger/INVESTIGATE/CRITICAL. Thresholds sourced from specs/obd2-research.md safe ranges. Never hardcode values -- always via config.json or spec-referenced constants
Footer shows: last sync timestamp (human-readable, e.g., '3m ago' or 'never'), total drive count, battery SOC + power source (external/battery/unknown)
Renderer accepts state via dataclass or dict -- no global state. State holders for connection-indicator values, min/max history, sync-recency, drive count

Tests cover: three-indicator state matrix (green/gray/red per indicator), color-coded value at each threshold tier with representative readings, min/max marker formatting, footer rendering with current + empty data

Simulator runs produce a visually recognizable advanced-tier screen (Ralph captures a PIL screenshot to tests/pi/display/ as a regression artifact OR describes the output precisely in the completion note)

SPOOL GATE 2 REVIEW PACKET: at story close, Ralph drops a note in offices/tuner/inbox/2026-04-18-from-marcus-us165-gate2-review.md (or a Ralph-authored equivalent) containing: threshold-to-color mapping table (param, blue-band, white-band, orange-band, red-band, source citation per band), min/max marker design screenshot/description, connection-indicator design, Spool review request
Completion note: test counts, simulator-driven screenshot/description, Spool inbox note filename
verification: Simulator smoke: python src/pi/main.py --simulate --dry-run and screenshot/describe the advanced-tier output
invariants: Do NOT hardcode threshold values in Python code -- all bands come from config or spec-referenced constants
Do NOT add physical-display-only features (touch events, HDMI-specific rendering) -- that's US-183
Do NOT introduce global state -- renderer takes state as arguments
No regression on US-164 basic-tier rendering -- advanced tier extends, does not replace
Thresholds default MUST match specs/grounded-knowledge.md safe ranges exactly -- Spool owns any deviation
stopConditions: STOP if threshold values don't exist in specs for a gauge -- e.g., Boost has no clear danger band documented. Surface to PM (Spool inbox request) rather than inventing a number
STOP if the advanced-tier layout doesn't fit the 3.5" screen cleanly -- 480x320 is tight; file a note in the Spool packet rather than cramming
STOP if US-187 hasn't landed and you'd be editing src/pi/obd/ -- wait for US-187 (hard sequencing) to avoid rename conflicts

stories.title: pygame HDMI render validation on OSOYOO 3.5" touchscreen
stories.intent: Validate the pygame HDMI display path end-to-end on the physical OSOYOO 3.5" touchscreen connected to chi-eclipse-01. Sprint 10 deferred this from US-178 Option C (ACs rewritten to driver-mode validation, deferred pygame HDMI + touch to a future story). Now unblocked: TD-014 closed (HardwareManager boots), US-180 closed (UpsMonitor real data flows). Touch interaction is US-174 scope (Sprint phase) -- this story is render-only.

filesToTouch: src/pi/display/manager.py or driver.py (if HDMI+pygame driver needs setup paths polished)
src/pi/display/screens/ (only if render needs a full-screen wrap for HDMI-native output)
scripts/validate_hdmi_display.sh (new -- CIO-runnable driver)
tests/pi/display/test_hdmi_render.py (new -- Pi-only, pygame stub or smoke)
docs/testing.md (HDMI validation walkthrough)

acceptance: scripts/validate_hdmi_display.sh is a CIO-runnable driver. Steps: SSH gate -> verify display detected (tvservice -s or equivalent on Pi 5) -> launch Pi app in simulator mode with full-screen pygame -> render for N seconds (default 30) -> optional screenshot capture -> exit clean

CIO visually confirms: advanced-tier screen (from US-165 if landed, basic-tier otherwise) renders on the physical 3.5" screen at 480x320 without tearing, clipping, or crash. Both orientation and text readability pass CIO eyeball review
CIO confirms: no frame-rate stall -- at least basic animation (gauge needle or clock refresh) visible
CIO confirms: app exits cleanly on SIGTERM / pushbutton long-press without leaving the display in a bad state (no frozen frame, no black screen that requires reboot)
If advanced tier (US-165) has not landed yet, validate basic tier (US-164) only -- story does not block on US-165 but benefits if it lands first
Test: Pi-only pygame init smoke (ensures pygame.display.init() succeeds on the Pi aarch64 with SDL2 backend Sprint 10 validated)
Test: simulator-driven render produces expected PIL surface snapshot (mock pygame if needed for CI)
Known-issue log: if any pygame warnings or SDL warnings surface during render, capture to docs/ or a completion note -- Sprint 13+ can triage
Completion note: CIO validation checklist filled out, any observed glitches, Pi display server config sample (/boot/firmware/config.txt HDMI settings), pygame version + SDL version
verification: pytest tests/pi/display/ -v on Pi (pi_only marker for HDMI-specific tests; should run because the test is pi_only by design)
invariants: Do NOT remove or modify existing driver-mode code from Sprint 10 US-178 -- this story adds HDMI+pygame validation, does not replace other modes
Do NOT require touch interaction -- that's US-174 Sprint phase. This story is render-only
Do NOT call sudo from Python -- any display permission issues go in the driver script (bash) or config.txt, not in Python
All rendering goes through the existing primary_renderer surface -- do not fork a separate HDMI-only renderer
stopConditions: STOP if the 3.5" screen doesn't light up at all -- could be HDMI cable, Pi firmware, or physical display issue. CIO triages before Ralph continues
STOP if pygame init fails on Pi with SDL error -- Sprint 10 validated SDL 2.28.4 worked, but HDMI output adds a framebuffer dependency. Document, don't mask
STOP if simulator data doesn't produce visible motion on-screen -- suggests the render loop isn't actually being called or the display is stuck on first frame
